title: multi-tenant/v1 — 429 quota response envelope
description: JSON Schema for the response body returned (with HTTP status 429 and a Retry-After header) when a tenant exceeds its configured rate budget or concurrent-mission cap. Declared in v2.0 (executor MUST NOT emit from quota machinery at this tier). Emitted in v2.2 when isolation is enforced. The envelope is a JSON-RPC 2.0 error response; the multi-tenant/v1 extension specifies the shape of the error.data object.

id.description: The id of the originating JSON-RPC request, echoed per JSON-RPC 2.0 §5.

code.description: Sandbox-reserved JSON-RPC application error code for tenant quota exhaustion. Chosen to fall outside the JSON-RPC reserved range (-32768 to -32000) and outside A2A core error codes.

message.description: Short human-readable summary. Implementations SHOULD use 'Tenant quota exceeded' for consistency.

tenant_id.description: The tenant whose quota was exceeded. MUST equal the request's Message.metadata.tenant_id, or 'default' if the request omitted it.

quota.description: Which quota was exceeded. 'rate' = dispatch rate (token bucket). 'concurrency' = concurrent non-terminal missions (leaky bucket).

retry_after_seconds.description: Number of seconds the client SHOULD wait before retrying. MUST equal the integer value sent in the Retry-After HTTP header on the same response.

limit.description: The configured limit that was exceeded. For 'rate', requests per window. For 'concurrency', maximum non-terminal missions. MAY be omitted by operators who consider the configured value sensitive.

window_seconds.description: The window over which the rate limit is measured, in seconds. Present when quota = 'rate'. MAY be omitted by operators who consider the configured value sensitive. Absent when quota = 'concurrency'.

$comment: Transport-level requirements (HTTP 429 status, Retry-After header) are specified in spec.md §4.3 and cannot be expressed in JSON Schema. Validators of this schema should be paired with HTTP-level assertions in conformance harnesses.
